title: tecophilaeaceae
intro: The Tecophilaeaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, which is a taxonomic rank above the genus level. This family is composed of approximately 80 species, primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere, particularly in North America and Eurasia. The Tecophilaeaceae family includes plants with unique characteristics, such as narrow leaves and funnel-shaped flowers, and is often associated with alpine or grassland habitats. The plants within this family, including species like Chionographis and Tecophilia, exhibit a range of adaptations that enable them to thrive in diverse environments.
